Gas-phase harmonic molar heat capacity of the DGEBA monomer at 300 K from GFN2-xTB (xtb 6.7.1) frequencies, 0 imaginary modes; the MaterialsCodeGraph polymer-vertical eval baseline (mcg/evals/paper_targets.yaml, dgeba_cp300_gfn2, molecular_thermo.cp_harmonic_J_per_molK at 300 K = 397.7 J/(K mol), measured 2026-07; the eval tolerance of 8 J/(K mol) covers xtb-version drift in low-frequency modes).

Butane gauche-to-anti forward barrier (peak-minus-reactant along the CI-NEB minimum-energy path) from the AtomisticSkills chem-neb-barrier example (.agents/skills/chem-neb-barrier/examples/butane_conformer/output/neb_results.json), MACE-OFF23-small, 7 images, converged; forward_barrier_eV 0.12280791644116062, TS dihedral -120.28 deg

Epoxide ring-opening cure step (aromatic amine): glycidyl phenyl ether + aniline -> 1-(phenylamino)-3-phenoxy-2-propanol. GFN2-xTB (xtb 6.7.1) gas-phase reaction enthalpy at 298 K over conformer-searched global minima; the MaterialsCodeGraph eval baseline (mcg/evals/paper_targets.yaml, cure_dh_gpe_aniline_gfn2, reaction_thermo.dh298_kJ_per_mol = -106.3 kJ/mol = -1.101722 eV per reaction event, measured 2026-07). Literature calorimetric window for epoxy-amine addition: -95 to -118 kJ/mol per epoxide.

Epoxide ring-opening cure step (aliphatic amine): glycidyl phenyl ether + methylamine -> 1-(methylamino)-3-phenoxy-2-propanol. GFN2-xTB (xtb 6.7.1) gas-phase reaction enthalpy at 298 K over conformer-searched global minima; the MaterialsCodeGraph eval baseline (mcg/evals/paper_targets.yaml, cure_dh_gpe_methylamine_gfn2, reaction_thermo.dh298_kJ_per_mol = -116.1 kJ/mol = -1.203292 eV per reaction event, measured 2026-07). Literature calorimetric window for epoxy-amine addition: -95 to -118 kJ/mol per epoxide.

Landauer conductance G(300 K) from the committed MESCAL artifact reference/milestoneA/conductance.dat (carbon bilayer tutorial), T=300 row verbatim: "300   0.333222233511797 0.333222233511797" (two identical columns: the landauer.pl oracle vs the JAX MESCAL port). Unit nW/K, stated explicitly in the mescal repo (README quickstart "tr.conductance([300.0])  # nW/K"; mescal/mescal/transport.py:14 and eskm-tools/eskm_tools/landauer.py lines 1,16: "THz in, nW/K out", "Ballistic thermal conductance in nW/K"); converted to canonical W/K (x1e-9) = 3.33222233511797e-10 W/K. Validation provenance: I. Duchemin, D. Donadio, Phys. Rev. B 84, 115423 (2011), DOI 10.1103/PhysRevB.84.115423; MESCAL manuscript in preparation.

Closed-form Nan-EMT effective conductivity of the reference DGEBA epoxy + 5 vol% graphene-nanoplatelet draft from the materialscodegraph composite tool (mcg/tools/composite/emt.py effective_kappa, ported verbatim to frontend/src/lib/emt.ts): matrix km=0.2, filler k11=1200 (in-plane) and k33=6 (through-plane) W/(m K), platelet d1=5um and d3=0.02um (aspect d3/d1=0.004, oblate), interface conductance G=25 MW/(m^2 K), volume fraction f=0.05, randomly oriented. Kapitza radius a_K=km/G=8.0 nm. Pinned at kappa_random=1.2452 W/(m K) by the mcg eval target (mcg/evals/paper_targets.yaml composite_kappa.kappa_random_W_per_mK, tolerance 0.001), cross-pinned in frontend/src/lib/emt.test.ts and mcg/tests/tools/composite/test_emt.py. Validated formulas: C.-W. Nan, R. Birringer, D. R. Clarke, H. Gleiter, Effective thermal conductivity of particulate composites with interfacial thermal resistance, J. Appl. Phys. 81, 6692 (1997); spherical-limit cross-check D. P. H. Hasselman, L. F. Johnson, Effective thermal conductivity of composites with interfacial thermal barrier resistance, J. Compos. Mater. 21, 508 (1987).

source.detail: Acoustic ballistic-staircase anchor of the bulk-Si golden curve: T12 = 2.99992757 at nu = 0.05 THz (first grid point; the three acoustic lead channels are open, per-atom 0.005859375 = 3/512, staircase deviation |T - 3| = 7.24e-5, within the recorded ballistic_max_dev and the integer-staircase invariant of eskm_tools.invariants.check_run), read verbatim from the committed MESCAL artifact reference/fixtures/level2_bulk_si_n8.npz (arrays nu_thz, T12, n_channels, per_atom).
